title: citrus neocaledonica
intro: Citrus neocaledonica, also known as the New Caledonian papeda, is a species of citrus tree native to New Caledonia, which is characterized by its unique and edible fruit with a rough, warty skin. The medicinal properties of Citrus neocaledonica include its high content of limonene, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of consuming Citrus neocaledonica include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Citrus neocaledonica include tea and juice, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
